_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in developing workforce and position qualification standards.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building and developing high-performing data and AI teams, including talent acquisition, onboarding design, performance management, career development planning, and succession preparation.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-009 (LS, cosine: 0.569), LS-T-006 (LS, cosine: 0.568).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.1/10 (raw 0.511). Shared vocabulary: [developing, skill]. DCWF-only terms [position, qualification, standards, workforce] vs WIDAI-only terms [acquisition, ai, building, career, data, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.889 (P=0.911, R=0.869). P exceeds R by 0.043 — the DCWF element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.7%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in developing workforce and position qualification standards." and "Skill in building and developing high-performing data and AI teams, including ta". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.570 > STS 0.511 (gap +0.059). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [developing, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.4%. Shared tokens: [developing, skill]. DCWF-only: [position, qualification, standards, workforce]. WIDAI-only: [acquisition, ai, building, career, data, design].
